description: Targeted strategy: generates wrappers that map the full v8 API surface, enabling migration from v5 or v6 to v8.

mappings.notes: Custom assertion classes that used Execute.Assertion must be rewritten to use the new AssertionChain pattern in v8.
Global equivalency configuration moved from EquivalencyAssertionOptions to EquivalencyAssertionConfiguration in v8.

mappings.description: ActionAssertions.Should() removed in v6; use FluentActions.Invoking()
BeEquivalentTo gained optional config parameter in v8
mappings.notes: The config parameter is optional so existing calls still compile, but the signature changed.

mappings.description: AsyncFunctionAssertions return type changed in v6
mappings.notes: Return type changed from AsyncFunctionAssertions to NonGenericAsyncFunctionAssertions in v6. Wrapper type references must update.

notes: This strategy generates wrappers targeting the v8 API surface. Members removed in v8 (EquivalencyAssertionOptions) are excluded, and v8-only members (AssertionChain, Satisfy, EquivalencyAssertionConfiguration) are included. Use this when migrating a codebase to v8 exclusively.
